{"id":"project-LICHEN-5a8t","title":"Silent hash set overflow loses telemetry without indication","description":"## Location
lichen/samples/lora_ping/src/main.c:51

## Description
When the seen_hashes array fills up (64 unique packets), track_hash() silently ignores subsequent unique packets. No counter tracks how many unique packets were dropped, and no warning is logged. This causes unique_hashes_seen to plateau at 64 even if hundreds more unique packets arrive, making the telemetry misleading for long-running tests.

## Failure Scenario
Run the ping test for extended period where >64 unique packets are received. After the 64th unique packet, metrics.unique_hashes_seen stops incrementing. User sees '64 unique packets' but actual count could be 200+, with no indication data is being lost.

## Category

## Description
When the seen_hashes array fills up (64 unique packets), track_hash() silently ignores subsequent unique packets. No counter tracks how many unique packets were dropped, and no warning is logged. This causes unique_hashes_seen to plateau at 64 even if hundreds more unique packets arrive, making the telemetry misleading for long-running tests.

## Failure Scenario
Run the ping test for extended period where >64 unique packets are received. After the 64th unique packet, metrics.unique_hashes_seen stops incrementing. User sees '64 unique packets' but actual count could be 200+, with no indication data is being lost.
